title: Freedom Galactic News
content:
{{Top|ncl}}
{{Unlicensed}}
{{Quote|Be Where The Story Is Happening|Motto of Freedom Galactic News|<ref name="Casus Belli 89">{{CasusBelliCite|89|La Nouvelle République : Une autre époque pour Star Wars}}</ref>}}
'''Freedom Galactic News''' was a newsfeed established during the later [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] by a group of [[Journalist/Legends|journalists]] committed to preserving their autonomy from the governments of the day.

Freedom Galactic News operated from deep space, and was based aboard the ''[[Hearst]]'', a modified [[Class VI bulk freighter|Class VI freighter]]. The headstrong attitudes of the team members often resulted in clashes in the confined environment of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]], and the old ship's limited power output meant that she could not broadcast while under way—a drawback for a team whose motto was ''Be Where The Story Is Happening.''

Nevertheless, in a short time, the agency became one of the best-known in the Galaxy, renowned for its coverage of events on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].

==Personnel==
*'''[[Willy Shatner|Willy "Double Star" Shatner]]''' (''c''. [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]]–). The [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Hearst'' was a passionate fan of the [[Holodrama/Legends|holodrama]] ''[[Space Track]]''; affectionate but fatherly, he treated the crew and production team as a surrogate for the family he had lost in the destruction of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].
*'''[[Fred Varley|Fred "Long-Short" Varley]]''' (''c''. [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]–). The [[Freighter/Legends|freighter's]] young, loudmouthed [[Corellia/Legends|Corellian]] navigator wore loud, fashionable [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] clothes that coordinated with his unabashed arrogance.
*'''[[Anselme Miclyne]]''' (''c''. [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]]–). Known as "Crude" (as in oil), the engineer aboard the ''Hearst'' was a cigar-smoking [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]], and a rare example of a solitary member of his [[Species/Legends|species]]. He had no time for strangers—or for the rest of the crew, either. His true love was the ship's drives, although, judging by the contents of his toolbox, they really were held together with spit, chewing-gum and bailing wire.
*'''[[Géraldine Fleitcher]]''' (''c''. [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]–). A scoop-hungry reporter with something to prove—namely, her independence from her father, the [[Coruscant Galactic Media]]'s mogul [[Graham Fleitcher]]; her quarrelsome character disguised the fact that she was the brains of Freedom Galactic News.
*'''[[Jeremy de la Rochetaillée]]''' (''c.'' [[49 BBY/Legends|49 BBY]]–). A high-living veteran reporter, and sometime lover of Geraldine Fleitcher; although he successfully passed himself off as a [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] aristocrat, he really grew up his father's remote homestead on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].
*'''[[Tony Bellaroso]]''' (''c''. [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]]–). Known by the strange nickname "Ilm," the newsfeed's one-man technical army was energetic, impulsive, and perhaps rather vain. His ambition was to some day find himself in front of the camera, and to win fame as the presenter of his own top-billing show.

==Appearances==
